videos.title: Tel Aviv: The Don'ts of Visiting Tel Aviv, Israel
videos.description:
Are you heading to Tel Aviv? The beaches, the food scene, the people walking around looking gorgeous and fit? There are so many fantastic things to see and do in Tel Aviv from walking on the boardwalk along the beach to the museums to taking in all the Bauhaus architecture. Here we have the do's and don'ts of visiting Tel Aviv. What not to do in Tel Aviv from Safety to fun to food and so much more!
Filmed in Tel Aviv, Israel

Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCFr3sz2t3bDp6Cux08B93KQ/join

#telaviv #telavivbeach #israel 
Copyright Mark Wolters 2023

Learn how to plan your travels like we do with our Travel Planning 101 Course: https://www.brighttrip.com/woltersworld 

Grab some Wolters World travel gear http://www.woltersworld.store

Help Us Keep Make More Honest Travel Videos: https://www.patreon.com/woltersworld 

Hey There Fellow Travelers! Thank you for watching our honest travel vlogs from all over this wonderful world. If you would like to get in contact with us please follow us & send us a message via our social media channels below. Also, if you like our travel videos please feel free to share them with other fellow travelers. 

You can find us all over the internet:
Travel Advice & Destinations: https://www.youtube.com/woltersworld
Food Travel Videos: https://www.youtube.com/woltersworldeats 
Travel Tips: https://www.youtube.com/woltersworldshorts
Business Education: https://www.youtube.com/professorwolters
Tiktok: https://www.tiktok.com/@woltersworld 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/woltersworld
Jocelyn's Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jocelynwoltersworld 
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/woltersworld
Twitter: https://www.twitter.com/woltersworld 
Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/company/wolters-world-travel-culture/
videos.transcript: Hello fellow travelers, I'm here with Wolters' World and today we're here in Tel Aviv, Israel. Today we have for you are the don'ts of visiting Tel Aviv. I want to start with this one because when people come to Israel, they think, "Oh, it's just holy sites. It's just religious stuff about Christianity and Judaism and Islam." No, they have to realize that you come to Tel Aviv, don't think of it as religious city. Think of it more as a party and get to the beach kind of city because it is a totally different vibe here in Tel Aviv versus Jerusalem or if you're going to other cities around Israel. So, I want to get that out of the way. This is not part of your religious trip. This is part of your maybe if you're playing to the sun gods and you want to enjoy a really great time at the beach, that's what you're doing here in Tel Aviv, okay? So, I want to make sure you realize that. Don't think this is going to be the big religious city on your Israel vacation because that's Jerusalem. And they do celebrate Shabbat here, you know, the Saturdays where everything's closed. They do celebrate that here in Tel Aviv, but it's not as like you won't notice it as much as if you're in Jerusalem and other parts of the country. And whether you're a sun worshiper or not, one thing I want to tell you is don't skip out on the beaches and walking on the beachfront promenade. I mean, I'm here early morning and I can look down and there's people running along the beach. I mean, it's just a gorgeous run, but also the beach after beach after beach. What's cool is don't think there's not a beach for you cuz there's beaches for families, there's beaches for singles, there's beaches for all kinds of people, but the honesty, the beaches here are gorgeous and that promenade is something you don't want to skip out on. So, don't just enjoy the city and the food and the nightlife and the clubs and the bars and the our museums. No, no. Make sure you're hitting the beach, but honestly, don't worry. I mean, that's what people come here for. And when you are on those beaches, I want to say this, don't ignore the flags and the kind of warnings about where you should swim and not swim cuz you'll notice there'll be the flags out, you know, as if you can swim or not. But what's nice is they actually have got these buoys around certain parts you go to so you know where to swim cuz there actually is kind of a strong current out there. So, you want to be you know, kind of vigilant about where you are swimming just to be safe. And of course, when you're in a beach town, obviously don't forget your sun protection, your hats, your sunblock, you're reapplying quite often. But one thing I think people don't realize is you know, when people think of going to a beach city like Tel Aviv, they only put the sunblock on at the beach. What you have to realize is you're going to walk around the city to see the museums, the Bauhaus, and the architecture, and all kinds of stuff. And you get burned that way, too. So, do remember that, okay? Safety first when it comes to the sun. And speaking of walking around the city, I think an important don't not to forget is don't forget to go to the Carmel Market, okay? That is the big market, the shuk, you know, the the market. You're going to go there, and yes, it'll have the sights, the smells, the tastes, all the really good look snacks you want to have when you're here in Israel. They have that there. You want to get your your your your shirts for your kids? That's where you're going to go. So, don't miss out on that. And if you want kind of like a nicer market, cuz the Carmel Market's like the old school kind of thing where you're walking around, you really feel like, "Oh, this is really really cool." And you're here like you get that really good nice vibe. But, if you want like higher-end food, you can go to the Sarona Market, uh which is a little bit farther away. It's not as easy to get to or you it's not as much part of the I'm walking around seeing the city. You're kind of going more towards the east part of the city. You go see that. So, do be aware of that one. And the thing is, when you go to the Carmel Market, another don't I have for you is don't forget to negotiate at the Carmel Market. Now, when you're walking around Tel Aviv, look, when you see a price tag on you know, on food in a store or on a you know, a dress in a store, those prices are set, okay? The only time you're really negotiating really is actually at the market. So, don't be afraid to do that. And when you are negotiating at a market, remember, they're going to give you whatever price. You have to know in your mind, "What is that worth for me?" And don't go above that. Cuz here's the thing, a lot of those things you see at the market, you can get at other stalls. So, just keep walking around, and don't buy the first thing you see, because honestly, there'll be other ones there, and you can always go back around and find it again. Now, one of the big reasons why Jonathan actually wanted to come to Tel Aviv was the Bauhaus movement. And so, my don't for that is don't begrudge the Bauhaus buildings. So, if you're not sure what the Bauhaus style is, it's a type of architecture. When you're walking around like oh, if you're going down uh Rothschild Boulevard, you see those like curved balconies there? That's the Bauhaus movement, or you might call the International Style. And you have all these really cool architectural buildings around the city that you'll Some are kept up, some are a little bit more lived in, but you definitely see the vibe of how there's all this kind of mid-century modern architecture throughout Tel Aviv. Because Tel Aviv is a very new city. I believe it was, you know, formed in like 1909, and then in 1920s, 30s, they started to grow, and then the Bauhaus movement came, and it really expanded with that. So, just notice you're going to see that architectural style. You're like, "Hey." And you kind of get that seated again and again and again as you go through the city, or at least more of the near the beach part. Because honestly, when you're out by the airport and coming in, it's all glass and steel structures, so it's not as much fun. So, that's what I would say is, you know, don't stay out in the suburbs, though you can, because you can get in. There's, you know, the buses here, the the light rail, there's all kinds of stuff to get into town. But if you're going to be coming for the beaches, I I recommend don't skip out on staying at the beaches, because honestly, there's tons of hotel options to be around here. Now, no matter where you're staying, I mean, I don't want to tell you this, don't think it's just Bauhaus and beaches when you are here in Tel Aviv, because there's a fantastic food scene when you're here. Because I'll be honest, after you've been in Israel for about 10 days on a tour, I'm just saying, you get a little tired sometimes about the food, and you want to spice it up a little bit, try something a little bit different. But here in Tel Aviv, you get to have that from other places as well. So, it's kind of like a nice mix of food to go here. That's what I want to tell you. If you get a chance, don't pass up the chance to do a food tour when you're here in Tel Aviv, because there's so many good restaurants, and so many different types of food you can have here. So, you can get the culture, not just the beach. That's why people like coming here because Look, I can come here with my family. They got good stuff to do with your kids from the beaches and around the town, but also I can get culture with the museums. I can learn about the architecture, the Bauhaus, I mean, the Bauhaus Center you can go and learn about not just the the architectural style of Bauhaus, but also the art that went with it and the interior design that goes with it. All that mid-century modern, everything. I mean, it's really a cool place. And I'll be honest to you, I was surprised how much I did really like Tel Aviv. Just how chillax it was to hit the beach, go get a beer, you know, have some food along the way, chat with some locals. It was just a really fun place. And so, that was really fun, okay? And And honestly, when you're going around, I just want to add this one because this is one thing I talk about in our Don'ts of Israel video as well, but I just really want to really enforce that. Don't think leaving Israel is as fast as entering Israel. Cuz when you fly in, honestly, when you fly into Tel Aviv, it's a super efficient airport. Went very quickly for me. You're going to get off your plane. You're going to go to these little kiosks. You put your passport in there. They take a picture and they print out this little blue card for you, okay? No, you need to give yourself plenty of time to get back. Sometimes it is quick, sometimes it isn't, okay? So, don't think it's going to be the same. Don't let that entry quickness reflect and and influence your quickness of getting to the airport the day you fly. Honestly, don't mess around when it comes to timing when it comes to flying out of Tel Aviv because no one tells you. Don't forget there's a pre-security check. So, before you get to check your bags and check in, you have to go through a security check as well where they say hi and talk to and answer. They'll ask you some questions. So, that's why I mean, 3 hours is no joke. I'm being more comfortable, maybe four. I mean, so just something to think about when you're coming here. Now, the thing people worry about when they come to Israel is about safety. I'll be honest with you, when you're here in Tel Aviv, you feel pretty safe. Like we were walking late night with the kids, no problem. Here at the beach early in the morning, not worrying about it. That's just really cool. It's like you actually do feel really safe when you're here in Tel Aviv. And that's a nice thing. So, it actually helps you to relax, to enjoy those beaches, to enjoy yes, all the hummus and all the other great food you have when you're here. So, everybody's a part of that. So, you actually will see that. And And don't be afraid to actually ask them questions or like, hey, if you need directions. That's one of those things. Now, if I'm going to talk about safety when we are here, I mean, I know we already mentioned the sun for a safety thing, but another safety I have for you is don't drive or at least don't tempt the drivers when you're here in Tel Aviv. When you're in Israel, the drivers are a bit forward, let's say, a bit aggressive. And so, you can rent a car and you can drive around Israel, it's fine. But just know that the traffic and like that aggressive nature of getting into different spots and swerving in and and merging and stuff, it can be a bit stressful. Cuz unless you're used to driving that, it can be a bit stressful. So, so don't think that pedestrian street, i.e. sidewalk, is just for you because there's going to be other people riding around there. Tel Aviv, I mean, it's already an expensive country and it's here at a beach, you know, tourist destination. videos.title: Tel Aviv Israel Travel Guide: 13 BEST Things to Do in Tel Aviv,
videos.description:
How to find cheap flights and travel more: https://travelforalmostfree.com/ref=beforeyougo2 // Here are the best things to do in Tel Aviv.

=======================
Recommended Tours in Tel Aviv:

Day Tour to Jerusalem and Bethlehem from Tel Aviv: https://www.viator.com/tours/Tel-Aviv/Day-Tour-to-Jerusalem-and-Bethlehem-from-Tel-Aviv/d920-5209JERBETH?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-telaviv-desc

Nazareth and the Sea of Galilee Day Trip from Tel Aviv: https://www.viator.com/tours/Tel-Aviv/Nazareth-Tiberias-and-the-Sea-of-Galilee-Day-Trip-from-Tel-Aviv/d920-5209NAZA?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-telaviv-desc

Caeserea, Rosh Hanikra and Acre Day Trip from Tel Aviv: https://www.viator.com/tours/Tel-Aviv/Caeserea-Rosh-Hanikra-and-Acre-Day-Trip-from-Tel-Aviv/d920-5209CAESER?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-telaviv-desc

Masada and the Dead Sea Day Trip from Tel Aviv: https://www.viator.com/tours/Tel-Aviv/Masada-and-the-Dead-Sea-Day-Trip-from-Tel-Aviv/d920-5209MASDS?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-telaviv-desc
=======================

Welcome to our video on the top things to do in Tel Aviv, Israel's vibrant and bustling coastal city. Whether you're interested in culture, history, food, or just soaking up the local atmosphere, Tel Aviv has something to offer for everyone.
 
First on our list is the beaches. Tel Aviv boasts a long stretch of coastline with several beautiful beaches. Whether you're looking to relax and soak up the sun or take a dip in the Mediterranean Sea, the beaches of Tel Aviv are the perfect place to do it. Popular beaches include Gordon Beach, Frishman Beach, and Hilton Beach.
 
Next up is the Carmel Market. This bustling outdoor market is a sensory overload, with colorful stalls selling everything from fresh produce to clothing and souvenirs. Sample some local snacks, grab a fresh juice, and soak up the atmosphere of this vibrant market.
 
For those interested in history, a visit to the Independence Hall Museum is a must. This is the site where Israel's Declaration of Independence was signed in 1948, and the museum features exhibits and artifacts that tell the story of Israel's founding.
 
Another must-see historical site is the ancient city of Jaffa, which dates back to the Bronze Age. Stroll through the winding alleys and enjoy the beautiful architecture, art galleries, and restaurants. Don't miss the famous Jaffa Clock Tower, one of the city's most iconic landmarks.
 
If you're a foodie, Tel Aviv won't disappoint. The city is famous for its street food scene, with countless food trucks, stalls, and markets serving up a diverse range of cuisine. Don't miss the chance to try some local favorites like falafel, hummus, and shakshuka.
 
For art lovers, the Tel Aviv Museum of Art is a must-visit. This impressive museum features a collection of Israeli and international art, including works by Picasso, Dali, and Chagall.
 
Last but not least, don't miss the opportunity to take a stroll along Rothschild Boulevard, one of the city's most iconic streets. The boulevard is lined with cafes, restaurants, and shops, and is home to some of the city's most beautiful examples of Bauhaus architecture.
 
There you have it, our top picks for the best things to do in Tel Aviv. So what are you waiting for? Start planning your trip today and discover all that Tel Aviv has to offer.

Keep watching to see the top things to do in Tel Aviv, top attractions in Tel Aviv, places to visit in the Tel Aviv and Middle East travel guides.

area and Marvel at the unique style of each building with their streamlined design elements in vibrant colors if you're into history be sure to visit the independ attendance Hall of Tel Aviv along space that should not be missed during your visit to Tel Aviv robine square is not just an ordinary Square as it's home to Tel Aviv City Hall and serves as a venue for National events protests and setic is Tel aviv's first neighborhood and it still retains a lot of its original charm it's no surprise that it's considered one of the best neighborhoods in Tel Aviv with its Rich history beautiful biblical city of Nazareth located in Israel's Galilee region only two hours north of Tel Aviv Nazareth is the largest Arab City in Israel and is a fascinating blend of cultures the Nazareth Old City videos.title: From TLV Airport to Tel Aviv. Taxi or Train? (Tips from a Tour Guide)
videos.description:
If you’re planning to travel from TLV airport to Tel Aviv, then this video is going to provide you with some very important insights.

www.travelingisrael.com
https://www.rail.co.il/

For all my tips, accommodation recommendations, digital tours and booklets, check out my internet site - https://www.travelingisrael.com/shop-products/

Like my content? Want to support my work? Become a member and get early access to my videos as well as other perks!
Support me on Ko - fi - https://ko-fi.com/travelingisrael

0:00 - Intro
0:40 - Taking a Taxi
1:38 - Taking the train
2:40 - Important warning! 
3:38 - Taking the train to Tel Aviv step by step
7:47 - Tel Aviv insights 
 



---
Follow me...
ko-fi - https://ko-fi.com/travelingisrael
Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/travelingisrael
Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/travelingisrael/
TikTok - https://www.tiktok.com/@travelingisrael1
videos.transcript: If you’re planning to travel from TLV airport to Tel Aviv, then this video is going to provide you with some very important insights. I will start with a misconception that many people have. Just because you land at Tel Aviv airport, it doesn’t mean that you need to start your trip in Tel Aviv. Israel is tiny and the airport is located outside of the city, so you can easily take the train to Jerusalem as well. It won’t take you any longer. You could also rent a car and drive to the desert or the Galilee. Two hours and you are there. But this video is about getting to Tel Aviv, so let me show you how to do it. There are two ways: trains and taxis. If you decide to take a taxi, you need to be aware of scammers. They are the ones who will approach you in the arrivals hall or once you’ve exited the airport and ask you if you need a taxi. They won’t use a meter and they’ll definitely rip you off. Which isn’t the ideal way to start your trip to Israel. You need to go to the official taxi rank. Let me show you how to get there. You head out of the airport, turn left and walk all the way to here. Why is it to the side and not right in front of you when you come out? Good question, I don’t know. The other option is to take the train. The train is fast, cheap and convenient but it doesn’t run on Shabbat, so from Friday afternoon to Saturday night. It’s the same with Jewish holidays and the trains don’t usually run at night either. How can you be sure if the train is running when you land? Click the link below for all the information. Paying for your train ticket is really easy. You can pay by cash, credit card, Rav Kav or via different apps. I have already made videos about the apps and the Rav Kav, so be sure to check them out. I’ll just remind you that inside the welcome hall there’s a public transportation booth where you can purchase a Rav Kav. The ride to Tel Aviv takes 15 minutes and costs 9 shekels, so around 3 dollars. Now this is very important, so listen carefully. In Tel Aviv there are four stations. The first one is called Tel Aviv HaHagana. Now if you look at the map, you’ll see that the main bus station in Tel Aviv is right next to it and you might think, great, I will take the train and then the bus from the main bus station. Well, I have news for you. Think again. The area around the central bus station in Tel Aviv is not somewhere you want to be walking around. Tel Aviv is rich, Tel Aviv is the capital of high tech in Israel, but you will see none of that in the HaTachana HaMercazit (central bus station) area. You can walk around there during the day but I would advise solo female travelers to stay away from that area. Men shouldn’t go there at night either. The next two stations are HaShalom and Savidor. I assume that HaShalom will be closer to your hotel but Savidor is also in the center. The last station is Tel Aviv University, so I doubt you’ll be needing to go there. So let’s see how long it takes me to get from the airport to drinking at a cool yet overpriced café close to the beach. By taxi it should take 50 minutes and cost about 150 shekels so about $45 Now I’m going to take the train and you’re coming along with me for the ride. Now it is … I had to wait for … minutes until the train arrived. Now I know I just said to try to avoid HaHagana station but I need to be in south Tel Aviv today because I’m doing a welding course so I need to get out there. As you can see, south Tel Aviv isn't the nicest side of the city So it’s now 12:50 and it took me exactly one hour to get here. I’m drinking an overpriced Tel Avivan iced coffee and just being very cool. Now a few important words about Tel Aviv. If you’ve watched this video up until now, you are probably planning a trip to Tel Aviv. As a guide, I like to say that there are no two cities in the world like Jerusalem and Tel Aviv – they are so close geographically and yet so different. Tel Aviv is on the shore, Jerusalem is high up in the mountains. Jerusalem is old, Tel Aviv is young. Tel Aviv is rich and secular, and Jerusalem is poor and of great religious significance. In Jerusalem, there are sites that you just have to see, in Tel Aviv you don’t have to do anything – you can just lie on the beach and drink your martini. On Instagram and Tiktok you can find loads of good tips if you’re looking for cool cafes or neighborhoods, like Neve Tzedel or Florentin, and everyone will tell you to visit Levinski Market or HaCarmel Market. That being said, if you want to go to the beach and soak up a pleasant atmosphere, you can do that in any number of other destinations that aren’t as pricey: Greece, Italy, Wisconsin, Spain… I do think it’s worth your while to take tours in Jaffa and Tel Aviv. On my Tel Aviv and Jaffa video tours and in my booklet, I talk about the history of Jaffa, which is actually an ancient port city, and the history of Tel Aviv but I also like to talk about Israel. After all, the state of Israel was established in Tel Aviv. In Jerusalem, it’s all about the heavy history and religious sites: Abraham, King David, Jesus, the Temple… it doesn’t get any heavier than that. As I see it, Jerusalem is more about the Jews, and Tel Aviv is more about the Israelis. Anyway, you will find all the information you need about the train, Rav Kav, and my tours on my website. See you there! Yalla bye!

safetyNotes: 2:40 - Important warning
If you decide to take a taxi, you need to be aware of scammers
Now this is very important, so listen carefully
Now I know I just said to try to avoid HaHagana station but I need to be in south Tel Aviv today because I’m doing a welding course so I need to get out there

videos.title: FIRST TIME GOING TO ISRAEL 🇮🇱 WHAT YOU MUST KNOW and How To Behave and Plan Your Day in Tel Aviv
videos.description:
✅ GET MY TRAVEL TO ROME GUIDE 👉🏼 https://allgoglobal.com/guides-italy
💥 Best Restaurants 💥 Best Hotels to Stay 💥 Best Things to do in ROME 👉🏼 ALL IN ONE PLACE 

📧 MY EMAIL ADDRESS
AnnaGoldmanGlobal@gmail.com - I will do my best to help you 💜

✅ MY SIGNATURE RELOCATION COURSE ➡️ https://edu.allgoglobal.com/relocation 🙌🏻 
💥 Planning to move to another country? Let’s make YOUR RELOCATION as smooth and successful as possible! I will be happy to help with EVERY STEP of YOUR MOVE! 

🏆 BOOK A PRIVATE CONSULTATION WITH ME ➡️ https://calendly.com/annagoldman/private_session

📣 For business inquiries, you can reach me at anna.goldman.travel@gmail.com 

Today we are in israel! In Tel Aviv! 
Let’s have a super cool day as a first time visitor in Israel and Tel Aviv. We will start with traditional Israeli breakfast, visit a few amazing places in Tel Aviv and finish it off with a traditional, local KOSHER dinner

🚨‼️ My best global payment card: Revolut
https://www.revolut.com

⚠️ MY TRAVEL GUIDE TO ROME, ITALY 🇮🇹  👉🏼 
https://allgoglobal.com/#rec567908316

‼️Do you want to plan Your Next Trip together? Let’s a have a private consultation 🤗
📧 Reach out 👉🏼 Anna.Goldman.travel@gmail.com 
and let’s chat privately! 

⏱️⏱️⏱️ Time Codes: 
00:00 Welcome to Tel Aviv, Israel 🇮🇱 
00:38 First Time in Israel or Tel Aviv in December 
01:00 Vital Information about Israel - for First Time Visitors
01:49 Traditional Israeli Breakfast 
04:06 Tel Aviv Promenade 
05:36 How to Dress in Israel 
06:27 Sarona Market in Tel Aviv 
08:17 Traditional Israeli food: Pitta
09:21 Old Town Jaffa - Flea Market. In Tel Aviv
11:04 Traditional Kosher Meal in Israel 
12:06 Tel Aviv Night Life 

1 - first time in Israel! 
It’s Tel Aviv in December! I don’t need to say much here just look around! It’s so sunny and it’s 22 degrees Celsius outside daytime. 
Night time, well it will drop to pleasant 16 maybe. But never below 15. 

Quick super important one! 
Time difference between continental Europe and Israel 1 hour. Europe 1 hour behind. 
The UK is 2 hour behind. 

Local currency is Israeli shekel. 

2 - Israeli Breakfast 

Always leave a tip to your waiters in Israel! This is their actual living .. 
their salaries are very low and they are relying on tips a lot.
10% is an absolute must 
15% would be average - this is how much I usually leave 
20% is quite generous but please go for it if you feel like the service you received was outstanding! 

Breakfast - price - ILS 74 (Israeli Shekel) = $22

3 - Tel Aviv promenade - Israel 

People walk, jog, ride bikes, or sit on benches and fill their lungs with fresh air, all while being surrounded by a glorious 8.7 mile stretch of open views, blue horizons, and white sails bobbing on the waves.
 
4  - How to Dress in Israel 🇮🇱 

Whilst being generally a liberal country, one of the best tips I can give you is to be mindful of what you wear in Israel. Don’t dress vulgarly because there are lots of holy sites in Israel that a proper attire is required. Avoid wearing shorts or crop tops. Furthermore, when you visit a mosque you should wear a scarf or hijab to cover your head. 

5 - lunch in Tel Aviv  - Sarona market 

https://saronamarket.co.il

Sarona Market is the largest indoor food market in Israel. You can find there about ninety stores, kiosks, and restaurants.

7 — Flea market in Jaffa / Old Jaffa 

Jaffa is the ancient port city from which Tel Aviv has blossomed – a harmonious blend of the past and present. Visiting the Jaffa Flea Market, Shuk Hapishpishim, is a must when in Israel. 

The flea market itself is open Sunday through Thursday (10 am – 6 pm) and Friday (10 am – 2 pm). But the area is bustling 24/7 with some of the best bars and restaurants in the city. Or maybe you fancy discovering Jaffa in the light of the moon? 

8 - dinner in Tel Aviv 
GIVE IN TO ISRAELI FOOD AND EXPERIENCE A KOSHER MEAL 
Meat Kitchen, Tel Aviv 👇🏼
https://instagram.com/meatkitchen.tlv?igshid=MWI4MTIyMDE=

Basically, kosher means to be pure and proper for consumption and is linked to the Jewish tradition and the Torah. The thing about kosher is that you can’t combine certain types of food, particularly meat and dairy. 

You should definitely give it a go because it was amazing! It’s definitely one of those experiences that you need to have when visiting Israel.

9 - night life  - Tel Aviv comes alive at night so you should definitely enjoy the nightlife – it is considered as one of the best in the world.

🆓 DO YOU HAVE ANY QUESTION ABOUT TRAVEL TO NORWAY, living in Norway or travel in general?
Send me a message, I’ll be happy to help 🤗

Stay in touch! Stay safe! Be happy!

videos.title: Tel Aviv in 5 minutes | Travel Guide | With Day Trip to Jerusalem
videos.description:
IMPORTANT NOTICE - please read first, then comment!!!

Over a year has passed since we visited Tel Aviv - a city full of life, culture, diversity and creativity. But shortly after our trip, everything changed. The war in Israel brought suffering, uncertainty and fear. During this time, it didn't seem right to show carefree travelling moments when so many people were facing pain and loss.

Nevertheless, after careful consideration, we decided to publish our video now. Not because we ignore the difficult reality, but because we believe it is important to show the other side: the vibrant, open and inspiring Tel Aviv. A city where people of different backgrounds, religions and lifestyles come together to live, create and enjoy together.

This video is a glimpse of the Tel Aviv and Israel we experienced - full of joie de vivre, hospitality and creativity. It is a sign of hope and a reminder that behind the headlines are real people and places that are more than a conflict.

We know that the situation remains complex and painful, and we do not want to trivialise suffering or take political positions. Our aim is to show a piece of the beauty and diversity that make up this city and this country - and perhaps to remind people of the importance of understanding and humanity in difficult times.

Thank you for walking this path with us.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
Original text (from September 2023):

Tel Aviv. A colorful, lively city between tradition and modernity. A hotspot for young people and cosmopolitan travelers. With 300 days of sunshine a year, a great vacation is guaranteed here. Tel Aviv in 5 minutes is a travel guide including the beste attractions and a day trip to Jerusalem.
► Important information: Video was recorded in September 2023.

At Carmel Market, you can find souvenirs for your family and friends back home. Also sweet mountains of halva, baklava and kadaifi, colorful fruit and juice stands, spice vendors and a variety of street kitchens. We continue through the artists' quarter Florentin to the old town of Jaffa. It really is very beautiful. You can get an impression of the history and culture of Israel on a walk. 

The coast of Tel Aviv stretches for 14 kilometers with its numerous beaches. Hilton Beach is particularly popular with the rainbow community. Tel Aviv has generally made a point of being particularly LGBT-friendly. Gay Pride is also celebrated here every year.

We take a day trip to Jerusalem. It has the highest density of religious buildings in the world. The Church of the Holy Sepulchre is one of the largest shrines of Christianity. Jesus is said to have been crucified and buried here. Jews from all over the world come to the Wailing Wall to pray and commemorate. And on the Temple Mount is the Al-Aqsa Mosque, which is important for Muslims.

The next day, we take a day trip from Tel Aviv in a rental car. Driving on highways 1 and 90 through the West Bank is generally permitted, but this can change at any time. Our first destination: Masada National Park. According to legend, Herod built a palace here more than 2000 years ago. Nearby is the Dead Sea, the lowest point on earth.

We recommend a 5-7 day trip to Tel Aviv, if it is safe to travel to Tel Aviv. You can book your apartment here: https://bit.ly/Tel-Aviv-Trip *
